header.title: Hi, {firstName}
header.description: Here's your current financial snapshot. You can adjust your income, expenses and goals any time.

stats.stressHintHigh: Focus on the essentials this month.
stats.transactionsHint: Number of one-off expenses this month
stats.analyticsCtaBody: Fixed vs variable, per-category budgets, opportunities, history — all in /expenses/analytics.
stats.goalsEmptyHint: Set your first budget in detailed analytics.
stats.potentialSavingsEmptyHint: No prioritised savings opportunity detected this month.
stats.potentialSavingsHint: ≈ {yearly} per year — estimate, not a guarantee
stats.potentialSavingsBlockedHint: Complete your data first for a reliable projection.

completeness.emptyState: Profile complete — every number on this page can be trusted.

assistant.title: Complete my financial profile
assistant.description: Toggle the rows that apply to you and enter the monthly amount. You can edit everything later under /expenses.
assistant.toggleAria: Include {name} in the list to save
assistant.savedToast: {count, plural, one {# expense saved} other {# expenses saved}}.

learned.tone_gentle: You respond best to a gentle, encouraging tone.
learned.challenge_overspending: You watch out for surprise spending.
learned.challenge_no_savings: You want to build regular savings.
learned.challenge_debt: You want to regain control of your debts.
learned.challenge_fixed_costs: You keep an eye on fixed costs.
learned.trigger_stress: Stress can trigger spending for you.
learned.trigger_boredom: Boredom can trigger spending for you.
learned.trigger_late_night: Late-night purchases can catch you out.
learned.trigger_payday: Spending tends to peak around paydays.
learned.trigger_weekends: Weekends are a watch zone for you.

progress.new_expenses: {count, plural, one {# new expense recorded} other {# new expenses recorded}}
progress.new_goals: {count, plural, one {# new goal added} other {# new goals added}}
progress.new_budgets: {count, plural, one {# new budget set} other {# new budgets set}}
progress.new_memory_entries: {count, plural, one {# new note saved by the coach} other {# new notes saved by the coach}}

coachButton.title: Talk to my advisor
coachButton.subtitle: Ask a question, review your situation, get a recommendation.

complete_profile.title: Complete your financial data
complete_profile.bodyLow: Analysis reliability is low ({score}%). Add insurance, subscriptions and telecom to get precise recommendations.
complete_profile.bodyMedium: Profile almost complete ({score}%). A few major categories still missing before the coach can be fully precise.

budget_over.title: Cut your {category} spending
budget_over.body: You're over budget on {category} by {amount} this month (limit {limit}). The first lever to pull.

high_variable_share.title: Rein in your one-off spending
high_variable_share.body: Your variable spending hits {share}% of income this month. A simple cap can free up real margin.

low_emergency_fund.title: Build your emergency fund
low_emergency_fund.body: You only cover {months} months of expenses. A monthly auto-transfer is often the right starting point.

high_fixed_ratio.title: Audit your fixed costs
high_fixed_ratio.body: Your fixed costs are {share}% of income. Review insurance, subscriptions and telecom for possible cuts.

high_insurance_share.title: Compare your insurance premiums
high_insurance_share.body: You pay {amount}/month in insurance ({share}% of income). Useful to compare premiums and check your deductible.

high_subscriptions_share.title: Clean up your subscriptions
high_subscriptions_share.body: {amount}/month of subscriptions. A few minutes of audit often frees real monthly margin.

audit_top_variable_category.title: Cap your "{category}" spending
audit_top_variable_category.body: {transactions} transactions in this category this month. A small cap is often enough to regain control.

dominant_category.title: Watch "{category}"
dominant_category.body: {category} weighs {share}% of total spending. The structural item to keep an eye on.

low_savings_rate.title: Set up automated savings
low_savings_rate.body: Your savings rate is low ({rate}%). A small monthly transfer is often enough to start the engine.

build_emergency_fund.title: Build your emergency fund
build_emergency_fund.body: You only cover {months} months of expenses. Aim for at least 1 month before any other goal.

set_first_goal.title: Set your first financial goal
set_first_goal.body: Everything's under control — the right moment to formalise a target (emergency fund, project, purchase).

continue.title: Keep going
continue.body: Your finances are balanced. Hold your habits — no action needed today.

weakest.none: You're holding the line — budgets respected, savings progressing, emergency fund in place.
weakest.budget: At least one budget is over this month — your first lever to pull.
weakest.savings: Your savings rate is low. A small monthly auto-transfer is often enough to start the engine.
weakest.emergency: Your emergency fund covers less than a month. Aim for at least 1 month of expenses before any other goal.

alerts.title: Alerts
alerts.emptyTitle: Enter your data to get going
alerts.emptyText: Add your income and expenses to see your first alerts — a few minutes is enough.
alerts.cashflowNegativeTitle: You're spending more than you earn
alerts.cashflowNegativeText: Identify 1 or 2 non-essential expenses to cut.
alerts.cashflowPositiveText: Consider automating a monthly savings transfer.
alerts.runwayLowText: Short-term goal: 1 month of expenses set aside.
alerts.runwaySolidText: Keep it intact, that's your peace of mind.
alerts.runwayBuildingText: Aim progressively for 3 months of expenses.

cashflowNegative.headline: You're spending about {gap} more than you earn each month.
cashflowNegative.bodyAvoidant: Uncomfortable to look at — that's normal. We'll go gently, one expense at a time.
cashflowNegative.bodyDefault: Good news: it's measurable, so it's addressable. We aim at one category at a time.
cashflowNegative.nextAction: Identify a recurring expense you can cut by about {targetCut} this month.

highDti.headline: Your repayments are {pct}% of your income.
highDti.body: Above 30%, they weigh heavily on your take-home. We can lower this ratio before targeting savings.
highDti.nextAction: List your loans by interest rate and concentrate on the most expensive one.

emergencyFundFirst.headline: Your first milestone: build one month of expenses aside.
emergencyFundFirst.bodyAnxious: A financial cushion reduces daily anxiety — even small, it changes everything.
emergencyFundFirst.bodyDefault: An emergency fund protects you from surprises before aiming at more ambitious goals.
emergencyFundFirst.nextAction: Schedule an automatic transfer of {monthlyTarget} at the start of the month to a separate account.

lowSavings.headline: You could probably free up around {estimate} per month.
lowSavings.body: Non-essential categories often represent 5-10% of expenses without you noticing. A quick review is enough.
lowSavings.nextAction: Review your active subscriptions and cut those you haven't used this month.

solidAcceleration.headline: You already save {pct}% of your income.
solidAcceleration.body: Your base is solid. You can speed up a goal, consolidate your emergency fund, or start putting part of your savings to work.
solidAcceleration.nextAction: Pick a 6-to-12-month numbered goal and aim part of your take-home at it.

intermediate.headlinePositive: You could save about {projection} over 12 months at your current pace.
intermediate.headlineNeutral: You're at balance — that's a healthy working base.
intermediate.bodyDirect: You have the base. Set a 90-day numbered target and act.
intermediate.bodyStructured: Set 1 measurable habit, give yourself a weekly milestone, measure in 30 days.
intermediate.bodyGentle: Little by little, simple adjustments compound. No pressure, just consistency.
intermediate.bodyMotivated: You seem ready to act. We can turn that energy into simple habits that stick.
intermediate.bodyCalm: Little by little, simple adjustments compound. The goal isn't perfection but consistency.
intermediate.nextActionStructured: Set 1 key metric (savings rate, runway) and track it weekly for 30 days.
intermediate.nextActionDirect: Pick 1 concrete financial habit and launch it this week — automatic transfer or shopping cap.
intermediate.nextActionCalm: Set 1 financial habit to test for 30 days — automatic transfer, shopping budget, or weekly review.

victories.stepOne: One plan step validated this week — every tier counts.
victories.stepsMany: {count} plan steps validated this week — solid consistency.
victories.activeFive: Steady presence this week — that's what makes the long-run difference.
victories.activeThree: Several active days this week — the rhythm is building.
victories.runwaySolid: Your emergency fund stays solid. Keep preserving it.
victories.savingsRateHigh: Your savings rate stays above 15% — that's rare and precious.
victories.activeOne: You came back this week. Consistency beats intensity.
victories.presence: You're here. That's already a first step — we resume gently.

priorities.cashflowNegative: Pick one expense to cut this week — start with the easiest.
priorities.noEmergency: Schedule an automatic transfer to a separate savings account.
priorities.planContinue: Continue your plan — the next step is in the Plan tab.
priorities.subscriptions: Review your active subscriptions — that's the highest-impact action.
priorities.twelveMonthGoal: Pick a 12-month numbered goal and aim part of your take-home at it.
priorities.trackingHabit: Log 1 or 2 expenses this week to keep the tracking reflex.

longInactive.headlineDirect: It's been a few days — let's check in?
longInactive.headlineStructured: A few days without tracking — let's resume gently.
longInactive.headlineCalm: We haven't seen each other in a few days. Want to ease back in?

tightMonth.headlineDirect: Your expenses exceed your income by about {gap}/month.
tightMonth.headlineCalm: A tight month — about {gap} more than your income.
tightMonth.bodyGentle: No panic. One expense at a time is enough.
tightMonth.bodyCalm: We can tackle one category at a time, no pressure.

solidProgress.headline: You're moving in the right direction.
solidProgress.body: Runway of {months} months and savings rate above 15%.
solidProgress.bodyNoExpense: Savings rate above 15% and no recurring charges — solid.

emergencyGap.headline: You don't have a dedicated emergency fund yet.
emergencyGap.body: Starting small is enough — automation does the rest.

planTeaser.emptyDescription: A 30, 60 or 90-day action plan based on your data.
planTeaser.subtitle: {days}-day plan · {done}/{total} steps

coachTeaser.title: Insights
coachTeaser.subtitle: Markers computed from your data.
coachTeaser.empty: Add some income and expenses to see your first insights.

texts.categoryShare: This category is {pct}% of your expenses ({amount}/month). Try −20% for one month?
texts.cashflowNegative: You're spending about {amount} more than you earn. We can dig into where to cut.
texts.savingsLow: Currently {rate}. Even a symbolic automatic transfer secures the habit.
texts.savingsGood: {rate} savings rate. You can speed up a goal or consolidate the emergency fund.
texts.emergencyLow: Less than one month of expenses set aside. First step: aim for 1 month.
texts.emergencyBuilding: {months} months covered. Next solid tier: 3 months.

danger.label: Strained
danger.description: We pick things back up step by step.

neutral.label: Progressing
neutral.description: A few adjustments will make the difference.

goalsSummary.title: Goals
goalsSummary.emptyDescription: Create your first financial goal to move forward step by step.
